temporal_description:
(00:00-00:01) A close-up shot reveals the word 'MURDER' written in a messy, red, crayon-like substance across the panels of a white paneled door. The lighting is dim and ominous, casting deep shadows. (Storyline: This opening shot immediately establishes a sinister and threatening tone, suggesting a crime has occurred or is about to occur.) (Audio: 1) Tone of speech: N/A. 2) Background sounds or music: A high-pitched, eerie, and suspenseful musical score begins to build.)
(00:02-00:03) A woman with dark hair, her face filled with terror, screams as she clutches a small child. She is wearing a grey cardigan and a watch on her left wrist. Her eyes are wide with fear and shock. (Storyline: This shot introduces the primary victims, establishing their state of extreme fear and vulnerability, which heightens the suspense.) (Audio: 1) Tone of speech: N/A. 2) Background sounds or music: The woman's high-pitched scream is the dominant sound, layered over the continuing suspenseful music.)
(00:04-00:05) A man with long, dark hair and a menacing expression lunges forward. He is wearing a red corduroy shirt over a plaid collared shirt. He swings a large, black-handled axe with force, striking the white door. (Storyline: This is the inciting incident of the scene, revealing the attacker and his violent intent. His action directly follows the ominous 'MURDER' sign, confirming a violent event is unfolding.) (Audio: 1) Tone of speech: N/A. 2) Background sounds or music: The sound of the axe hitting the door is loud and impactful, punctuating the tense music.)
(00:06-00:07) A second man, with long hair and a beard, wearing a dark purple shirt, is violently thrown backward. He crashes hard against a wall, his body slumping to the floor as he looks back in shock. (Storyline: This shot introduces another character who is also a victim of the attack, expanding the scope of the violence and showing the attacker's strength.) (Audio: 1) Tone of speech: N/A. 2) Background sounds or music: The sound of a body hitting the wall is heard, along with the ongoing tense score.)
